signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7865 — SNB Vice Chairman Schlegel confirms 'elevated willingness' to intervene in FX markets. Options market pricing extreme protection: IV at 12.8% vs HV30 at 6.9% generates a +5.9% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), the highest in the G10 space. Despite a +375bp carry advantage for USD (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%), the SNB's verbal pivot introduces immediate downside risk to the carry trade. Watch for a break of 0.7800 to confirm intervention-led reversal.

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/AUD at 1.8769 — G8 scorecard identifies a 1.165% performance spread between leader GBP and laggard AUD. GBP 1W avg +0.546% vs AUD 1W avg -0.618% confirms a strong bilateral theme. While AUD holds a +60bp carry advantage over GBP (RBA 4.35% vs BoE 3.75%), the technical and momentum divergence suggests institutional rotation out of AUD. Watch for RBNZ on 27 May to potentially spill over into broader Antipodean sentiment.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5880 — COT LF net short -19,437 (specs fading the kiwi) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the 27 May meeting. Speculative positioning is crowded at 22.3% long (extreme short), creating a high-conviction short-squeeze setup if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish hold or a surprise hike. Fed's Barkin noting policy is 'in a good place' limits USD upside, further exposing the spec short. Watch for a break above 0.5920 to trigger a stop-run.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.91 — 278bp OIS carry (SOFR 3.51% vs TONA 0.73%) remains the primary driver as VIX at 16.8 signals normal volatility. However, 25d Risk Reversals show a -1.32 put skew, indicating the options market is paying a premium for JPY strength (USD/JPY downside) despite the carry advantage. This reflects persistent hedging against BoJ intervention as price holds near 160.00. Carry dominates the regime until VIX exceeds 25.
